task_completion.description: Log in to the OpenCAD system as administrator
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenCAD dashboard is visible, indicating a successful login (e.g., admin user profile or welcome screen).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Police Dashboard (MDT) and open the Create Incident Report form
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Police Dashboard (MDT) interface is active, and a form to create a new incident report is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct title for the incident report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title input field contains exactly 'Vandalism - Dragline 4'.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct narrative for the incident report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The narrative text area contains the text: 'Upon 0800 perimeter check, discovered spray paint vandalism on the rear counterweight of Dragline 4. No suspects observed. Photos taken.'

task_completion.description: Set the report type to 'Vandalism' (or 'Other') and leave the civilian field blank/unknown
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report type dropdown or selection is set to 'Vandalism' (or 'Other'), and the civilian/suspect field is left empty or set to 'unknown'.

task_completion.description: Submit and save the incident report
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message/toast indicating the report was created, or the newly created report is visible in the list of active incident reports.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenCAD web GUI to create the report
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the web browser and OpenCAD interface, not terminal commands (like curl or mysql) injecting data directly into the database.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenCAD application
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows http://localhost and the interface matches a genuine CAD/MDT system, rather than a local text editor or mock HTML file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the success screen
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are not open and being used to alter the DOM to fake a success message or report list.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Title: 'Vandalism - Dragline 4'. Expected Narrative snippet: 'rear counterweight of Dragline 4'. Expected Type: 'Vandalism' (or 'Other'). The civilian field must be blank or unknown. The task must be performed within the OpenCAD web interface (Police Dashboard/MDT) at http://localhost.
